title: Demand-Driven Context: Teaching AI Agents to Remember What Matters
description: A framework for enterprise AI agents that lets problems drive context curation — like TDD for knowledge. Presented at AI Builders meetup in Amsterdam.

slides.title: Demand-Driven Context
slides.subtitle: Teaching AI Agents to Remember What Matters
slides.author: Raj Navakoti

slides.title: "I Have This Condition"
body: Zero memory of what happened ten minutes ago.
Without his system of tattoos and Polaroid photos — he's just a confused man in a motel room.
slides.notes: Remember Memento? Leonard Shelby — the guy who can't form new memories. Every morning he wakes up in a motel room with no idea how he got there.

slides.title: Your AI Agent is Leonard
body: It confidently produces a beautifully structured, completely wrong answer.
slides.notes: Give your agent a real enterprise problem and it wakes up like Leonard. Expert reasoning. Zero memory of your company.

slides.title: IKEA vs Your Enterprise
body: Physical world: Clear pathways, numbered sections, you walk in with a list and walk out with exactly what you need.
Digital world: Hundreds of systems, thousands of APIs, tribal knowledge locked in people's heads, docs scattered everywhere.
slides.notes: Anyone who's shopped at IKEA knows how easy it is to navigate. But look at the digital side of any enterprise. No clear path. No numbered arrows.

slides.title: We Have the Pipes, Not the Water
body: But who curates what flows through them?
slides.notes: The pipes are getting better every week. But there's a problem upstream: how do you curate the context? Most teams either dump everything in or guess. Both fail.

slides.title: The Curation Problem
slides.notes: Both extremes fail. There's a third way.

slides.title: What if the Agent Told You What's Missing?
body: Treat your AI agent like a new joiner on Day 1.
They don't read the entire wiki.
They start working on real problems and ask questions.

slides.title: DDC: Demand-Driven Context
items: Give the agent a real problem
Let it tell you what's missing
Let it ink its own tattoos
slides.notes: Problem-driven context curation. Like TDD for knowledge — RED, GREEN, REFACTOR.

slides.title: What Just Happened
items: RED — Agent searched KB → empty → produced Demand Checklist
GREEN — Human pasted rough notes → Agent curated 5 typed entities
DONE — Agent answered 3 vendor questions with real specifics

slides.title: Stop Documenting Everything
body: Give your agent a real problem.
Let it tell you what's missing.
Let it ink its own tattoos.
Unlike Leonard — your agent's tattoos are version-controlled.
